setting.note: The narrator's Harlem: a stretch of Lenox Avenue and 125th Street where he speaks for the Brotherhood, sees an old couple evicted onto the pavement, and is finally swept into a night of fire and looting. The arc runs from a Southern college and a downtown paint works into the heart of Harlem and down to the forgotten cellar where a man who feels unseen retreats underground.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Ralph Ellison, first published 1952. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Invisible Man, Ralph Ellison (1952). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The near-black of the coal cellar and of invisibility itself, the darkness the narrator lines with stolen light. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
